name: Toyol
alias: the spirit child · the little thief
body: A Toyol is a small green-skinned infant spirit, eyes wide and red, raised from a dead fetus by a bomoh and kept in a jar. Its keeper feeds it drops of blood from a pricked toe and sends it out at night to steal — money, gold, small valuables lifted from sleeping households who never feel a thing.

It has the mind of a toddler, easily delighted and easily fooled, which is its only weakness. A house that suspects a Toyol will leave out beads, marbles, or sand by the door; the creature stops to count and play and forgets why it came, and is gone by morning.
note: Sudden, unexplained good fortune in a neighbour was the classic accusation. Money kept beside a mirror or a needle is said to repel it. When the keeper dies, the Toyol must be passed on or it torments the family — which is how the jars travel.
